---
title: "Task Chooser"
---

# Task Chooser

Start from the work, not from the metaphor. Pick the closest task, copy the spell, then verify against the linked evidence path.

| Task                    | Start Here                                         | Verification Path                                                   |
| ----------------------- | -------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Refactor safely         | [spell](../spells/safe-refactoring.qmd)            | [evidence](../examples/evaluations/safe-refactoring.qmd)            |
| Diagnose an incident    | [spell](../spells/bug-diagnosis-from-logs.qmd)     | [evidence](../examples/evaluations/bug-diagnosis-from-logs.qmd)     |
| Design an API           | [spell](../spells/api-design.qmd)                  | [evidence](../examples/evaluations/api-design.qmd)                  |
| Migrate live data       | [spell](../spells/migration-without-data-loss.qmd) | [evidence](../examples/evaluations/migration-without-data-loss.qmd) |
| Generate tests          | [spell](../spells/test-generation.qmd)             | [evidence](../examples/evaluations/test-generation.qmd)             |
| Tune performance        | [spell](../spells/performance-tuning.qmd)          | [evidence](../examples/evaluations/performance-tuning.qmd)          |
| Red-team an AI workflow | [spell](../spells/jailbreak-resilience-review.qmd) | [evidence](../examples/jailbreak-resilience/index.qmd)              |

For higher-risk work, choose a stack after selecting the first spell. Release, migration, recursive decomposition, and AI red-team work should generally use stack workflows rather than one-shot prompts.
